title: Solving for x
description: Solve one-step equations by undoing the operation while keeping both sides equal.

scaffolded.title: Undo to set x free
scaffolded.body: To find x, get it alone. Do the **opposite** move to both sides so the scale stays balanced.

steps.text: x + 5 balances 12. A +5 rides with x.

gate.hint: To undo something that was added on, you take that same amount back off again.

steps.text: Subtract 5 from both pans to stay level.

gate.template: Take 5 off both pans and x ends up ___.
gate.hint: Once the extra 5 is lifted away, nothing else is left sitting with x on its pan.

steps.text: Every move has an opposite that undoes it.

gate.template: The move that undoes multiplying is ___.
gate.hint: Multiplying builds up equal copies, so to undo it you split the total back into equal groups.

scaffolded.prompt: x + 4 = 9. What should you do first to find x?
hints: To get x alone, undo the number that was added to it.
A 4 is added to x, so think of the opposite of adding 4.
Whatever undo you pick, do it to both pans so the scale stays fair.

feedback.correct: Yes! Taking 4 off both sides leaves x by itself.
incorrect: Do the same undo step to both sides.

scaffolded.title: Subtract from both sides
scaffolded.body: Watch the undo-by-subtracting move. Take the extra weight off both pans to free x.

interaction.resultCaption: Take 6 off each side: 14 becomes 8, so x balances 8 alone.

predict.prompt: Predict: take 6 off each pan and the right pan reads...

scaffolded.prompt: x plus 4 pounds balances 9 pounds. Get x alone, keep both pans balanced!

feedback.correct: Once x stands alone, it balances 5. x = 5!
incorrect: x is alone now, so read the remaining weight.

hints: The scale only stays balanced if both pans change together.
Take the extra 4 pounds off both pans to free x.
Once x is alone, read what is still on the other pan.

feedback.correct: Balanced the whole way, so x = 5.
incorrect: Only one side changed. Keep the scale balanced.
Remove 4 from both sides before choosing x.

scaffolded.prompt: x - 2 = 6 means x became 6 after losing 2. What is x?
hints: To undo a subtraction, add the amount back on.
x dropped to 6 after losing 2, so add that 2 back.
Think what number falls to 6 when 2 is taken away.

feedback.correct: Yes! You added it back, so x = 8.
incorrect: What number becomes 6 after you take away 2?

scaffolded.prompt: 3x = 12 means three matching x blocks make 12. What is x?
hints: Matching x blocks each hold the same amount.
Split the 12 evenly across the three x blocks.
Think what number, added three times, builds up to 12.

feedback.correct: Exactly! Three equal x's share 12, so each is 4.
incorrect: Three x blocks share the 12 equally.

feedback.correct: Right, add the 3 back and x = 8.

followUp.prompt: What if it were x - 6 = 5 instead? Would x be bigger or smaller?
followUp.why: x fell further down to land on 5, so adding the bigger amount back makes x bigger.

feedback.correct: Exactly, split 18 across 3 equal x's, so each is 6.

followUp.prompt: What if it were 3x = 24 instead? Would x be bigger or smaller?
followUp.why: A bigger total shared among the same three x's gives each one more, so x is bigger.
